I went for a walk this morning in Sam Smith Park. A good day for seeing year-round and winter regulars: red-necked grebes with white necks (non-breeding plumage), buffleheads, long-tailed ducks, a couple of coots, a kingfisher, gadwalls, red-breasted mergansers. Missed the snow buntings, alas.

BIRD ALERT! You might not know this about me: I’m an avid birder! Since I was a kid I’ve been coming to the shores of Lake Michigan, seeing some of my favorite birds each season and recharging in our beautiful wild spaces here in Illinois’ 9th District, including the Montrose Bird Sanctuary!🐦‍⬛🪺

Willets use their plumage to hide and blend in with the rocks or beaches that they can be found on. Willets have some of the earliest departure times south, starting to leave in late June. These Willets stuck around for an evening. They were here, until they weren't!

The Chicago bird killer is McCormick Lakeside Center. It's not tall, or lit up …but it has 1/3 mile highly reflective glass facing Lake Michigan. Birds coming off the lake head for the trees in front, see those trees and sky reflected, and continue thinking they can fly through.

I did. That bird is in all of its original paint. It was lost in Lake Michigan behind the training carrier USS Wolverine. The bird lost power on final and settled into the water behind the ship. The ensign got out and she sat on the bottom for 50 years. I had 15 images of that but I lost quite few

Figured it out. Benign. There are a lot of marshes & wetlands inland from Lake Michigan. The surface and groundwater gets drained to ditches & then to these pipes, & finally out to sea. The brown color is just tannin & other organics from decayed plant matter. Explains why the ducks don’t mind.
